tasks.subject: runtime-model-discovery [P1] Expand EffortLevelSchema and ModelOptionSchema in shared package
tasks.description:
Expand the shared Zod schemas to support the universal model capability schema. This is the foundational type change that all other tasks depend on.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Expand EffortLevelSchema

In `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`, replace the current `EffortLevelSchema` (line ~96):

**Current:**
```typescript
export const EffortLevelSchema = z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high', 'max']).openapi('EffortLevel');
export type EffortLevel = z.infer<typeof EffortLevelSchema>;
```

**New:**
```typescript
export const EffortLevelSchema = z
  .enum(['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'max', 'xhigh'])
  .openapi('EffortLevel');
export type EffortLevel = z.infer<typeof EffortLevelSchema>;
```

This is a superset covering Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and Grok effort levels. Existing values ('low', 'medium', 'high', 'max') remain valid.

### 2. Expand ModelOptionSchema

In `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`, replace the current `ModelOptionSchema` (around line ~1134):

**Current:**
```typescript
export const ModelOptionSchema = z
  .object({
    value: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Model identifier (e.g. claude-opus-4-6)' }),
    displayName: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Human-readable model name' }),
    description: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Short model description' }),
    supportsEffort: z
      .boolean()
      .optional()
      .openapi({ description: 'Whether this model supports effort levels' }),
    supportedEffortLevels: z
      .array(EffortLevelSchema)
      .optional()
      .openapi({ description: 'Available effort levels for this model' }),
  })
  .openapi('ModelOption');
```

**New:**
```typescript
export const ModelOptionSchema = z
  .object({
    // --- Identity (existing, unchanged) ---
    value: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Model identifier (e.g. claude-opus-4-6)' }),
    displayName: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Human-readable model name' }),
    description: z.string().openapi({ description: 'Short model description' }),

    // --- Reasoning (existing + expanded) ---
    supportsEffort: z.boolean().optional(),
    supportedEffortLevels: z.array(EffortLevelSchema).optional(),
    supportsAdaptiveThinking: z
      .boolean()
      .optional()
      .openapi({ description: 'Claude decides when and how much to think' }),

    // --- Speed variants (new) ---
    supportsFastMode: z
      .boolean()
      .optional()
      .openapi({ description: 'Whether this model supports fast output mode' }),
    supportsAutoMode: z
      .boolean()
      .optional()
      .openapi({ description: 'Whether this model supports auto mode' }),

    // --- Context (new) ---
    contextWindow: z.number().int().optional().openapi({ description: 'Maximum input tokens' }),
    maxOutputTokens: z.number().int().optional().openapi({ description: 'Maximum output tokens' }),

    // --- Provider metadata (new, for future multi-runtime) ---
    provider: z
      .string()
      .optional()
      .openapi({ description: 'Provider identifier (e.g. anthropic, openai)' }),
    family: z.string().optional().openapi({ description: 'Model family (e.g. claude-4, gpt-5)' }),
    tier: z
      .enum(['flagship', 'balanced', 'fast', 'specialized', 'legacy'])
      .optional()
      .openapi({ description: 'Model tier for UI grouping' }),

    // --- Capability flags (new, for future use) ---
    supportsVision: z.boolean().optional(),
    supportsToolUse: z.boolean().optional(),
    supportsStreaming: z.boolean().optional(),
    supportsCodeExecution: z.boolean().optional(),

    // --- Lifecycle (new) ---
    isDeprecated: z.boolean().optional(),
    isDefault: z.boolean().optional().openapi({ description: 'Suggested as default selection' }),
  })
  .openapi('ModelOption');

export type ModelOption = z.infer<typeof ModelOptionSchema>;
```

The `ModelOption` type is inferred from the schema. No separate type definition is needed. All new fields are optional, so existing code continues to work.

### 3. Run typecheck

After the schema changes, run `pnpm typecheck` to find any downstream breakages from the expanded `EffortLevel` type. The `AgentSession.effort` field in `agent-types.ts` currently uses a literal union `'low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'max'` instead of importing `EffortLevel` -- this will need to be updated to accept the full `EffortLevel` type (handled in Task 3.1).

Also verify that `onModelsReceived` in `message-sender.ts` (line ~54-62) still type-checks. The inline type there specifies `supportedEffortLevels?: ('low' | 'medium' | 'high' | 'max')[]` -- this should be updated to use the `EffortLevel` type import to stay in sync with the schema.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `EffortLevelSchema` has 7 values: 'none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'max', 'xhigh'
- [ ] `ModelOptionSchema` has all new fields: `supportsAdaptiveThinking`, `supportsFastMode`, `supportsAutoMode`, `contextWindow`, `maxOutputTokens`, `provider`, `family`, `tier`, `supportsVision`, `supportsToolUse`, `supportsStreaming`, `supportsCodeExecution`, `isDeprecated`, `isDefault`
- [ ] All new fields are optional (backward compatible)
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes across the monorepo (after updating inline type literals as needed)
- [ ] Existing `ModelOption[]` values (like `DEFAULT_MODELS` in `runtime-constants.ts`) still validate
tasks.activeForm: Expanding EffortLevelSchema and ModelOptionSchema in shared package

tasks.subject: runtime-model-discovery [P1] Add fastMode and autoMode to session schemas
tasks.description:
Add `fastMode` and `autoMode` fields to the session-related Zod schemas and the `UpdateSessionRequest` type. These are the shared type changes that enable the server (Phase 3) and client (Phase 4) session state expansions.

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Update SessionSchema

In `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`, add `fastMode` and `autoMode` to `SessionSchema` (currently around line ~99):

```typescript
export const SessionSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().uuid(),
    title: z.string(),
    createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
    updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
    lastMessagePreview: z.string().optional(),
    permissionMode: PermissionModeSchema,
    model: z.string().optional(),
    effort: EffortLevelSchema.optional(),
    fastMode: z.boolean().optional(), // NEW
    autoMode: z.boolean().optional(), // NEW
    contextTokens: z.number().int().optional(),
    cwd: z.string().optional(),
  })
  .openapi('Session');
```

### 2. Update UpdateSessionRequestSchema

In `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`, add `fastMode` and `autoMode` to `UpdateSessionRequestSchema` (currently around line ~125):

```typescript
export const UpdateSessionRequestSchema = z
  .object({
    permissionMode: PermissionModeSchema.optional(),
    model: z.string().optional(),
    effort: EffortLevelSchema.optional(),
    fastMode: z.boolean().optional(), // NEW
    autoMode: z.boolean().optional(), // NEW
    title: z.string().min(1).max(200).optional(),
  })
  .openapi('UpdateSessionRequest');
```

### 3. Verify type inference

The `Session` and `UpdateSessionRequest` types are inferred from their schemas via `z.infer<>`. The new fields will automatically appear on the inferred types. Run `pnpm typecheck` to confirm no breakages.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SessionSchema` includes `fastMode: z.boolean().optional()` and `autoMode: z.boolean().optional()`
- [ ] `UpdateSessionRequestSchema` includes `fastMode: z.boolean().optional()` and `autoMode: z.boolean().optional()`
- [ ] `Session` type (inferred) includes `fastMode?: boolean` and `autoMode?: boolean`
- [ ] `UpdateSessionRequest` type (inferred) includes `fastMode?: boolean` and `autoMode?: boolean`
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes across the monorepo
tasks.activeForm: Adding fastMode and autoMode to session schemas
